title: PRISM team config
description: Per-team config for token substitution and tech-stack-conditional content. Lives at .ai-skills/config.json in the consumer's repo. The onboarding flow (Atlas, Phase 2) writes this; agents read from it at chat time when behavior depends on tech stack. Derived tokens used at sync time but not stored as fields: ${PROJECT_LOWERCASE} (lowercase form of project, used for git-config namespaces), ${TICKET_PREFIX_LOWERCASE} (lowercase form of ticketPrefix). Add new derived tokens at the same seam in scripts/ai-skills/lib/tokens.ts (Phase 2).

org.description: Display org name. Used in PR descriptions, changelog headers, etc.

ticketPrefix.description: Ticket ID prefix used in branch names, commits, and PR titles.

ticketSystem.description: Ticket system configuration. `kind: "linear"` and `kind: "github-issues"` are supported; the object shape is the abstraction lever for future providers (e.g. Jira). The Linear-specific fields below apply only when `kind` is `linear`.

kind.description: Ticket system provider. `linear` carries workspace/teamKey; `github-issues` tracks work as GitHub issues with no Linear team.

projectId.description: Linear project UUID, if scoped to a single project.

teamKey.description: Linear team key. Often matches ticketPrefix.

github.description: GitHub repo info for Eric (PR review), Sage (changelog), Reese (QA plans), Lilac (standup).

defaultBranch.description: Default branch name. Most teams use `main`.

techStack.description: Tech stack flags. Drives onboarding's per-codebase rule generation — for each language and framework listed, Atlas generates a corresponding rules file populated from patterns in the team's actual code (Phase 2). Validated against an enum so the set stays curated; add new values to the enum when a real team needs them.

rules.description: Rule selection. `universal` is the always-included set (the universal rule set ships with PRISM). Language- and framework-specific rules are generated per-codebase during onboarding (Phase 2), not opted into via this field. `optIn` is reserved for future non-language opt-in rules.

optIn.description: Names of opt-in rule files (without .md extension).

slackChannel.description: Optional Slack channel for Lilac standup posting.
